claim: Durable execution with Temporal allows agents to resume from the exact point of failure after a crash, preserving all context and progress made before the crash
actionable: When building agentic systems, use Temporal's durable execution to automatically checkpoint state at each step so that failures don't restart the entire workflow from the beginning
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Melissa Herrera: Your Agents Should Be Durable

claim: Restarting a failed agentic workflow from the beginning wastes tokens already burned on LLM calls and loses progress due to agent nondeterminism
actionable: Avoid naive restart-on-failure patterns with LLM-based agents; instead implement durable execution frameworks that track state and resume from the last successful checkpoint
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Melissa Herrera: Your Agents Should Be Durable

claim: AI agents are distributed systems and should be treated as such; Temporal applies the same durable execution patterns traditionally used for distributed system failures to agentic workflows
actionable: When architecting agentic systems, apply lessons from distributed systems design: think of each agent step as a task in a distributed system and ensure failure isolation and recovery
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Melissa Herrera: Your Agents Should Be Durable

claim: Temporal workflows use three primitives: workflows (orchestration logic), activities (external requests/tool calls), and workers (compute layer executing code)
actionable: Structure agentic code using workflow-activity-worker separation: put orchestration in workflows, tool calls in activities, and run workers as compute nodes to achieve fault-tolerant execution
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Melissa Herrera: Your Agents Should Be Durable

claim: Temporal automatically handles crash recovery, retries, long-running tasks, and state management so developers can write simple happy-path code while getting enterprise-grade reliability
actionable: Use Temporal as an abstraction layer for production agents: write your agent logic as if it will never fail, and let Temporal handle retries, timeouts, and state persistence
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Melissa Herrera: Your Agents Should Be Durable

claim: OpenAI uses Temporal internally to orchestrate image generation workflows and Codex, demonstrating production-scale applicability to long-running AI tasks
actionable: When evaluating orchestration frameworks for production AI systems, note that Temporal is battle-tested at OpenAI's scale for image generation and code synthesis
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Melissa Herrera: Your Agents Should Be Durable

claim: Building production-ready AI agents requires handling long-running tasks, API rate limits, tool failures, and hardware failures—significantly more complex than the demo happy path
actionable: Don't assume a working agent demo will scale to production; plan for rate-limiting, network faults, and extended execution timelines from the start
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Melissa Herrera: Your Agents Should Be Durable
